question: Which of the following is most likely to cause problems to an amateur radio receiver in a vehicle?

question: A good reason for keeping a log of all transmission is because
options: It will make it easier to check allegations of interference.

question: Before transmitting you should first listen on a frequency and then ask if the frequency is clear because
options: the frequency may be in use but one of the stations may be in a location where you can't receive their signal.

this lets other amateur radio operators know that you are about to commence a QSO.

question: When calling "CQ, this is M7ABC calling CQ on 20 meters" you should
options: repeat the sequence several times in quick succession over a period of 20 - 30 seconds, then listen for several seconds
call the sequence once and then listen for several seconds in between each
repeat the sequence several times in quick succession, moving the transmit frequency up a few kHz each time.
repeat the sequence several times in quick succession, moving the transmit frequency down a few kHz each time

question: Once you have contacted another station on a VHF FM calling channel you should
options: remain on the frequency until another station wishes to use it.
remain on the frequency as long as the contact takes.
ask if the frequency is clear.

question: Band plans are used because

options: Excellent audio and very strong signal strength
Average audio and a very strong signal strength

options: may have the owners callsign embedded in the configuration.
automatically check to see if a channel is in use.

must be used with horizontal polarisation.

question: In the event that a fuse blows you should
options: find out the cause of the problem.
replace the fuse with one of a higher rating.
measure the voltage across the fuse.
return the affected equipment to the place of purchase.

question: A Residual Current Circuit Breaker with Overcurrent Protection (RCBO) will stop mains power in a circuit:
options: Only when there is a current leakage to earth above its leakage threshold.
Only when there is an excessive current in the circuit above the current threshold.
When there is both earth leakage and excessive current above the device threshold.
When there is either earth leakage or excessive current above the device threshold.

question: When carrying out maintenance or repair work on electronic equipment you should
options: follow the manufacturers guidelines for servicing the equipment.
ensure that the correct fuse is fitted to the mains plug.
ensure that part of you is in contact with the metal chassis.
have someone else present with you in the room.

question: The mains power switch to the radio shack should be
options: key operated to prevent use by non-licensed persons.

question: One feature of Nicad (NiCad), Nickel Metal Hydride (NIMH) and Lithium batteries is that
options: they all have different charging requirements and must only be used with the recommended charger.
the terminal voltages are all the same allowing almost any charger to be safely used.
they may be safely discarded in the household waste or a litter bin in the street.
they must be kept fully charged at all times to ensure a long life.

question: When using power tools it is
What safety considerations should you bear in mind when using power tools rather than hand tools?
options: Power tools can do more damage and don’t stop instantly.
Power tools can produce a neater and more accurate job.
It is a lot less effort when using power tools.

question: Soldering work stations:
options: Require an emergency cut oﬀ switch in case the soldering tip comes into contact with the skin.
Should be well ventilated to avoid inhaling fumes.

question: Why should two people be present during antenna erection?

Because there needs to be someone on the ground who can see what needs doing.

question: Overreaching when on a ladder may cause
options: the person to fall off the ladder
a hard hat to give less protection
a poorly constructed or loose fitting job

question: The main purpose of wearing a tool belt when up a ladder is to

avoid having to repeatedly climb the ladder

options: oversees the preparation of the International Radio Regulations
produces guidance on the safe levels of RF exposure

options: microwave radiation could overheat your eye
waveguides have very sharp edges and can cut
you could be accidentally stabbed in the eye

question: Antennas connected to transmitting equipment
options: should be mounted where they cannot accidentally be touched when in use.
should be placed where they can easily be manually adjusted.
must be connected with the shortest possible feeder.

options: measures should be taken to protect against lightning.

options: located away from overhead power cables.

asuitable amperage fuse should be installed at the generator to avoid electrocution.
the local council should be informed
Residual Current Circuit Breakers are not required.

question: Using excessive volume when wearing headphones can result in
options: complaints from neighbours about the noise

options: The location of antenna feeders and power cables.
